expectations.text: Prevalidates the candidate with --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, and one or more --baseline-path arguments
expectations.evidence: The answer never supplies the required candidate prevalidation invocation or any of the flags --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, or --baseline-path. Instead, lines 9-20 construct a SHA-256 path-to-hash map and validate a temporary card snapshot containing that map.

expectations.text: Passes the identical commit and path list as artifact_baseline in kanban_complete metadata
expectations.evidence: Lines 22-34 pass artifact_baseline as BASELINE, but BASELINE is explicitly the path-to-SHA-256 map defined in lines 9-18. No baseline commit plus path list is represented or passed, so the required identical commit/path baseline is absent.

expectations.text: Reruns the live-card harness after completion without candidate baseline overrides
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-43 say to refetch the card, then serialize it into a temporary card JSON 'exposing the persisted artifact_baseline at the card field the harness checks' and run the harness on that serialization. This is not a demonstrated live-card harness rerun without candidate baseline overrides, and no override-free command is provided.

expectations.text: Uses the persisted completion-run metadata as the post-completion authority
expectations.evidence: The post-completion authority in lines 36-43 is a refetched card's persisted artifact_baseline hash map and a derived temporary card JSON. The answer does not identify or consume persisted completion-run metadata as the authority for the post-completion harness run.

The answer directly substitutes per-file hashes: lines 9-18 require SHA-256 hashes for each committed and working-tree file, and lines 22-40 require that exact path/hash map to be persisted as artifact_baseline. This contradicts the assertion.
